title: Add Highway Code Citations and Road Traffic Act 1988 References in collapsible Legal Reference panel

requirements.summary: Add a static data structure (frontend-side) that maps each violation type to relevant Highway Code rules, including the rule number, description, and whether the rule is legally enforceable or advisory
acceptanceCriteria: Each violation type maps to at least one Highway Code rule entry
Each entry includes rule number, short description, and an enforceable/advisory flag
Advisory rules are visually distinguished from enforceable ones (e.g. label or badge)

file_operations.description: Create a static data structure that maps violation types to Highway Code rules. Each rule includes ruleNumber (e.g., 'HC 126'), description (short plain-English text), and isEnforceable (boolean flag). Use common UK road traffic violation types that align with the existing app's violation tracking (e.g., 'speeding', 'red_light_violation', 'dangerous_driving', 'careless_driving', 'mobile_phone_use', 'no_seatbelt', 'wrong_lane', 'failed_to_stop'). Export a typed constant mapping violationType strings to arrays of Highway Code rule objects.

requirements.summary: Add a static data structure (frontend-side) that maps each violation type to relevant Road Traffic Act 1988 sections, including the section number and a short plain-English description of the offence
acceptanceCriteria: Each violation type maps to at least one RTA 1988 section
Each entry includes section number (e.g. s.3, s.2) and a short description
Entries are accurate to common UK road traffic violation types already in the app

file_operations.description: Extend the legalReferences module to include a static data structure mapping violation types to Road Traffic Act 1988 sections. Each entry includes sectionNumber (e.g., 's.2', 's.3', 's.35') and description (short plain-English offence description). Use the same violation type keys as the Highway Code mapping. Export a typed constant for RTA 1988 references.

requirements.summary: In the ReportDetail view, add a collapsible 'Legal Reference' panel that is collapsed by default. When expanded, it shows the Highway Code Citations and Road Traffic Act 1988 References relevant to the violations recorded in that report
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Legal Reference' section is present in the ReportDetail view
The panel is collapsed by default with a visible toggle button/chevron
Expanding the panel reveals Highway Code rules and RTA 1988 sections relevant to the report's violations
If no violations are recorded, the panel shows a message indicating no legal references apply
The panel can be collapsed again after being expanded

file_operations.description: Create a collapsible LegalReferencePanel component that accepts a list of violations as props. The component is collapsed by default and displays a toggle button/chevron to expand/collapse. When expanded, it maps each violation's violationType to the corresponding Highway Code rules and RTA 1988 sections from the static data structures. Display Highway Code rules with rule number, description, and a badge/label to distinguish enforceable from advisory rules. Display RTA 1988 sections with section number and description. If the violations array is empty, show a message like 'No violations recorded, no legal references apply.' Use shadcn/ui Collapsible component for the toggle behavior and Card component for layout.

Import and integrate the LegalReferencePanel component into the ReportDetail view. Place the panel after the existing violations/liability sections but before the photo gallery. Pass the report's violations array as a prop to LegalReferencePanel. Ensure the panel is rendered for all reports, even those with empty violations arrays (the panel itself will handle the empty state).
